title: Hut 8 Commercializes First Phase of 1 GW Beacon Point AI Data Center Campus with 15-Year, 352 MW IT Lease with Base-Term Contract Value of $9.8 Billion
text: Hut 8 commercialized the first phase of its Beacon Point AI data center campus in Texas with a landmark 15-year lease agreement worth $9.8 billion in base-term contract value. The deal represents one of the largest AI data center contracts ever signed. Campus Scale: Beacon Point is designed to eventually support up to 1,000 MW of utility capacity, making it one of the most ambitious data center campuses in North America. The first phase of 352 MW represents the initial major commercial deployment. Tenant Profile: The tenant is a high-investment-grade company, indicating the quality and stability of the contractual relationship. The triple-net, take-or-pay lease provides Hut 8 with highly predictable cash flows. Contract Economics: Including renewal options, the total contract value could reach approximately $25.1 billion. The lease includes a 3% annual base rent escalator and flexibility for future expansions. Infrastructure: The campus is designed to NVIDIA's DSX reference architecture, optimizing it for AI training and inference workloads. Initial data hall delivery is expected in Q3 2027.
